column1: The most common cancer in children is Leukemia
column2: a cancer of the blood and bone marrow
column3: accounting for about 30% of all childhood cancers
column4: with Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) being the most frequent type. Brain and spinal cord tumors are the second most common
column5: followed by lymphomas and solid tumors like neuroblastoma and Wilms tumor
column6: according to sources from the American Cancer Society

column1: Leukemia: Starts in the bone marrow
column3: with ALL being the most prevalent form.

column1: Brain and Spinal Cord Tumors: These are the most common solid tumors in children
column2: often developing in the lower parts of the brain.

column1: Lymphomas: Cancers of the immune system (lymphocytes).
Neuroblastoma: A cancer of immature nerve cells

column2: often found in young children around ages 3-4.

column1: These cancers affect different parts of the body and have varied treatments
column2: but significant advances have greatly improved outcomes for many children
column3: notes the Pediatric Cancer Research Foundation and UC Davis Comprehensive Cancer Center.
